page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, which contains 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The instance is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics placing in the top 25% of tested servers. PassMark CPU and encryption tests also rank in the top 25%, reflecting robust cryptographic capabilities supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA-NI instruction sets. Memory bandwidth and general processing tasks, such as Geekbench software compilation and image processing, perform in the average range. Large-block memory write speeds reach 54.44 GB/sec, while LLM text generation for a small 135M model demonstrates strong performance at 306.54 tokens per second.

The primary tradeoff of the n2d-highcpu-48 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:1, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale caching layers. However, this configuration provides high compute density, making it a cost-efficient choice for CPU-bound workloads where memory overhead is minimal. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 48.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 24 physical cores. This configuration provides a lean 1.0 GB of memory per vCPU and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, with stress-ng CPU and PassMark encryption scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, while LLM text generation for small models is highly efficient. The low memory-to-CPU ratio represents a clear tradeoff, making this instance less suitable for memory-heavy applications. However, its high compute density offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound tasks. It is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong multi-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-48 features 48 vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, delivering strong CPU performance for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring forty-eight dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and a lean memory profile optimized for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 24-core AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs
Configured with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Delivers top-tier CPU performance in stress-ng and PassMark benchmarks
Lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware
Optimized for software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and web serving
